status.computeTimedOut: Compute failed: worker is still initializing

results.simulatedDescription: This shows how the selected picture would look through the current optical settings.
results.simulatedDescriptionWithTarget: This shows how the selected picture would look through the current optical settings. Current target: {{description}}
results.enlargementHint: Click the image to view it enlarged.
results.psfDescription: The rendered point spread function for the current optical system.
results.wavefrontDescription: The rendered wavefront map for the current Zernike aberration values.

logmar_chart.label: Eye Chart (logMAR)
logmar_chart.description: The first six lines of an eye chart, with letters arranged in rows.
logmar_chart.supplementalDescription: The PSF chart may show a clear intensity cutoff around the central region. This limit is intentional: it keeps chart generation responsive while reducing memory use and computational cost, without changing the underlying optical simulation.

snellen_e_20_20.label: Snellen Chart Letter E on 20/20
snellen_e_20_20.description: An eye-chart letter E from the 20/20 (6/6) line. An eye with standard vision should be able to resolve this target from 20 feet or 6 meters away.

jupiter.label: Jupiter (angular diameter 50 arcsecond)
jupiter.description: A picture of Jupiter used for showing how fine details are softened by aberrations.

point_source.label: Point Source (Airy Disc)
point_source.description: A single tiny bright point, useful for showing how a perfect dot spreads out.

siemensstar.label: Siemens Star
siemensstar.description: A circular pattern of black-and-white spokes, useful for showing defocus and astigmatism.

slantededge.label: Slanted Edge
slantededge.description: A tilted black-and-white edge used to show how crisp an edge looks.

tiltedsquare.label: Tilted Square
tiltedsquare.description: A rotated square used to show how corners and edges change.

aberrations.title: Optical Aberrations (Zernike)
aberrations.lowerOrder: Lower Order Aberrations (Generally Correctable with Ordinary Eyeglasses)
